commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blocker is maintainer/contributor runtime proof and cache-policy review, not a narrow automated code repair.; Cleared: The diff stays in Swift menu code/tests, adds no dependency or automation surface, and the second commit removes raw user-visible strings from height-cache keys. (sha=74528926113aa4dfb803acadfbb4ac1eb8fbf0a3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds content-fingerprinted menu-card height cache keys and focused tests so unchanged SwiftUI menu cards can reuse measured heights across menu invalidations.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence after-fix runtime reproduction is present here. Source inspection confirms the current version-scoped measured-height cache path, but the affected packaged macOS 26.x setup still needs proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is maintainer/contributor runtime proof and cache-policy review, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: Cache menu card heights by content
target.body:
Refs #1274.

## Summary
- keep menu-card height cache entries reusable across menu-content version invalidations when callers provide a width + content fingerprint
- add a `UsageMenuCardView.Model` height fingerprint and pass it through the merged, stacked, history, and chart menu-card call sites
- keep no-fingerprint callers version-scoped, with focused tests for stable content, changed content, legacy fallback, eviction, and provider scoping

## Why
The latest #1274 sample after #1297 points back to the shared menu rebuild core: `populateMenu -> addMenuCardSections -> MenuCardItemHostingView.measuredHeight`, where each cache miss allocates a fresh `NSHostingView` just to measure card height.

#1286 moved close rebuild work off the synchronous dismiss path, and #1297 targets icon-observation churn. This PR is the next separate slice: avoid repeating the expensive measured-height pass when card content and width are unchanged. It does not claim to close #1274; affected macOS 26.x / Merge Icons machines should still retest the packaged build.

## Claw/Copilot Follow-up
- replaced raw text cache-key fields, including account email, with per-process salted SHA-256 text shapes (`chars`, `utf8`, `lines`, digest prefix), so cache keys no longer retain raw PII or user-visible strings
- encoded optional string presence explicitly, including `storageText`, so `nil` and `""` no longer collapse into the same height identity
- pruned stale `version:` fallback keys on `invalidateMenus()` while retaining content-fingerprinted keys, avoiding old version-scoped entries accumulating until the global 256-entry flush
- made the height-cache tests use deterministic `AccountInfo(email: nil, plan: nil)` fixtures instead of `UsageFetcher().loadAccountInfo()`

## Validation
- `swift build`
- `swift test --filter MenuCardHeightFingerprintTests` (2 tests)
- `swift test --filter StatusMenuHeightCacheTests` (6 tests)
- `make check` (SwiftFormat lint + SwiftLint: 0 violations)
- `swift test` (3244 tests / 385 suites)

## Remaining Proof Gap
I still do not have affected macOS 26.x / Merge Icons packaged-app proof for the after-fix `measuredHeight`/`NSHostingView` runtime path. This PR should remain a targeted cache-policy slice, not the final closure of #1274, until an affected setup confirms the packaged branch.
target.author: hhh2210

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
